_comment: Watermark for the /dream learning-synthesis workflow. Per-profile state with a TWO-POINTER cursor over each profile's ~/.pi/<profile>/sessions tree, sorted by ISO8601 session timestamp (the header `timestamp` field / filename prefix), NEVER by id. Legacy sessions use random UUID4 ids that are not time-sortable, so an id-based cursor would let one old session exclude all newer UUID7 sessions. Each profile holds {cursor, floor}: `cursor` is the highest timestamp already processed (the new edge — forward runs take sessions strictly ABOVE it, newest-first); `floor` is the lowest timestamp processed contiguously (the old edge — backfill runs take sessions strictly BELOW it, newest-first, draining history toward the start). Each run is capped at MAX_SESSIONS_PER_RUN (see SKILL.md) so the first run and any heavy catch-up self-throttle: forward sweep takes priority, backfill drains the remaining backlog over subsequent runs. Empty cursor AND empty floor = first run (take the newest N, set cursor=batch max, floor=batch min). /dream only analyzes top-level session transcripts (excludes nested */run-*/* subagent transcripts), advances the pointers, and commits this file as part of the dream PR. This file is the cross-machine source of truth; it lives in the repo, not machine-local state.
